label: prophetic
text: Hear, O heavens, and give ear, O earth: for the LORD hath spoken, I have nourished and brought up children, and they have rebelled against me.

label: prophetic
text: The ox knoweth his owner, and the ass his master’s crib: but Israel doth not know, my people doth not consider.

label: prophetic
text: Ah sinful nation, a people laden with iniquity, a seed of evildoers, children that are corrupters: they have forsaken the LORD, they have provoked the Holy One of Israel unto anger, they are gone away backward.

label: prophetic
text: And he said, Go, and tell this people, Hear ye indeed, but understand not; and see ye indeed, but perceive not.

label: prophetic
text: Make the heart of this people fat, and make their ears heavy, and shut their eyes; lest they see with their eyes, and hear with their ears, and understand with their heart, and convert, and be healed.

label: prophetic
text: Then said I, Lord, how long? And he answered, Until the cities be wasted without inhabitant, and the houses without man, and the land be utterly desolate,

label: prophetic
text: And the LORD have removed men far away, and there be a great forsaking in the midst of the land.

label: prophetic
text: But yet in it shall be a tenth, and it shall return, and shall be eaten: as a teil tree, and as an oak, whose substance is in them, when they cast their leaves: so the holy seed shall be the substance thereof.

label: prophetic
text: Who hath believed our report? and to whom is the arm of the LORD revealed?

label: prophetic
text: For he shall grow up before him as a tender plant, and as a root out of a dry ground: he hath no form nor comeliness; and when we shall see him, there is no beauty that we should desire him.

label: prophetic
text: He is despised and rejected of men; a man of sorrows, and acquainted with grief: and we hid as it were our faces from him; he was despised, and we esteemed him not.

label: prophetic
text: Surely he hath borne our griefs, and carried our sorrows: yet we did esteem him stricken, smitten of God, and afflicted.

label: prophetic
text: But he was wounded for our transgressions, he was bruised for our iniquities: the chastisement of our peace was upon him; and with his stripes we are healed.

label: prophetic
text: All we like sheep have gone astray; we have turned every one to his own way; and the LORD hath laid on him the iniquity of us all.

label: prophetic
text: He was oppressed, and he was afflicted, yet he opened not his mouth: he is brought as a lamb to the slaughter, and as a sheep before her shearers is dumb, so he openeth not his mouth.

label: prophetic
text: He was taken from prison and from judgment: and who shall declare his generation? for he was cut off out of the land of the living: for the transgression of my people was he stricken.

label: prophetic
text: And he made his grave with the wicked, and with the rich in his death; because he had done no violence, neither was any deceit in his mouth.

label: prophetic
text: Yet it pleased the LORD to bruise him; he hath put him to grief: when thou shalt make his soul an offering for sin, he shall see his seed, he shall prolong his days, and the pleasure of the LORD shall prosper in his hand.

label: prophetic
text: He shall see of the travail of his soul, and shall be satisfied: by his knowledge shall my righteous servant justify many; for he shall bear their iniquities.

label: prophetic
text: Therefore will I divide him a portion with the great, and he shall divide the spoil with the strong; because he hath poured out his soul unto death: and he was numbered with the transgressors; and he bare the sin of many, and made intercession for the transgressors.

label: prophetic
text: Then the word of the LORD came unto me, saying,

label: prophetic
text: Before I formed thee in the belly I knew thee; and before thou camest forth out of the womb I sanctified thee, and I ordained thee a prophet unto the nations.

label: prophetic
text: Then said I, Ah, Lord GOD! behold, I cannot speak: for I am a child.

label: prophetic
text: But the LORD said unto me, Say not, I am a child: for thou shalt go to all that I shall send thee, and whatsoever I command thee thou shalt speak.

label: prophetic
text: Be not afraid of their faces: for I am with thee to deliver thee, saith the LORD.

label: prophetic
text: Then the LORD put forth his hand, and touched my mouth. And the LORD said unto me, Behold, I have put my words in thy mouth.

label: prophetic
text: See, I have this day set thee over the nations and over the kingdoms, to root out, and to pull down, and to destroy, and to throw down, to build, and to plant.

label: prophetic
text: The word that came to Jeremiah from the LORD, saying,

label: prophetic
text: Stand in the gate of the LORD’s house, and proclaim there this word, and say, Hear the word of the LORD, all ye of Judah, that enter in at these gates to worship the LORD.

label: prophetic
text: Thus saith the LORD of hosts, the God of Israel, Amend your ways and your doings, and I will cause you to dwell in this place.

label: prophetic
text: Trust ye not in lying words, saying, The temple of the LORD, The temple of the LORD, The temple of the LORD, are these.

label: prophetic
text: For if ye throughly amend your ways and your doings; if ye throughly execute judgment between a man and his neighbour;

label: prophetic
text: If ye oppress not the stranger, the fatherless, and the widow, and shed not innocent blood in this place, neither walk after other gods to your hurt:

label: prophetic
text: Then will I cause you to dwell in this place, in the land that I gave to your fathers, for ever and ever.

label: prophetic
text: Behold, ye trust in lying words, that cannot profit.

label: prophetic
text: Will ye steal, murder, and commit adultery, and swear falsely, and burn incense unto Baal, and walk after other gods whom ye know not;

label: prophetic
text: And come and stand before me in this house, which is called by my name, and say, We are delivered to do all these abominations?

label: prophetic
text: Is this house, which is called by my name, become a den of robbers in your eyes? Behold, even I have seen it, saith the LORD.

label: prophetic
text: And he said unto me, Son of man, stand upon thy feet, and I will speak unto thee.

label: prophetic
text: And the spirit entered into me when he spake unto me, and set me upon my feet, that I heard him that spake unto me.

label: prophetic
text: And he said unto me, Son of man, I send thee to the children of Israel, to a rebellious nation that hath rebelled against me: they and their fathers have transgressed against me, even unto this very day.

label: prophetic
text: For they are impudent children and stiffhearted. I do send thee unto them; and thou shalt say unto them, Thus saith the Lord GOD.

label: prophetic
text: And they, whether they will hear, or whether they will forbear, (for they are a rebellious house,) yet shall know that there hath been a prophet among them.

label: prophetic
text: And thou, son of man, be not afraid of them, neither be afraid of their words, though briers and thorns be with thee, and thou dost dwell among scorpions: be not afraid of their words, nor be dismayed at their looks, though they be a rebellious house.

label: prophetic
text: And thou shalt speak my words unto them, whether they will hear, or whether they will forbear: for they are most rebellious.

label: prophetic
text: The hand of the LORD was upon me, and carried me out in the spirit of the LORD, and set me down in the midst of the valley which was full of bones,

label: prophetic
text: And caused me to pass by them round about: and, behold, there were very many in the open valley; and, lo, they were very dry.

label: prophetic
text: And he said unto me, Son of man, can these bones live? And I answered, O Lord GOD, thou knowest.

label: prophetic
text: Again he said unto me, Prophesy upon these bones, and say unto them, O ye dry bones, hear the word of the LORD.

label: prophetic
text: Thus saith the Lord GOD unto these bones; Behold, I will cause breath to enter into you, and ye shall live:

label: prophetic
text: And I will lay sinews upon you, and will bring up flesh upon you, and cover you with skin, and put breath in you, and ye shall live; and ye shall know that I am the LORD.

label: prophetic
text: So I prophesied as I was commanded: and as I prophesied, there was a noise, and behold a shaking, and the bones came together, bone to his bone.

label: prophetic
text: And when I beheld, lo, the sinews and the flesh came up upon them, and the skin covered them above: but there was no breath in them.

label: prophetic
text: Then said he unto me, Prophesy unto the wind, prophesy, son of man, and say to the wind, Thus saith the Lord GOD; Come from the four winds, O breath, and breathe upon these slain, that they may live.

label: prophetic
text: So I prophesied as he commanded me, and the breath came into them, and they lived, and stood up upon their feet, an exceeding great army.

label: prophetic
text: Then he said unto me, Son of man, these bones are the whole house of Israel: behold, they say, Our bones are dried, and our hope is lost: we are cut off for our parts.

label: prophetic
text: Therefore prophesy and say unto them, Thus saith the Lord GOD; Behold, O my people, I will open your graves, and cause you to come up out of your graves, and bring you into the land of Israel.

label: prophetic
text: And ye shall know that I am the LORD, when I have opened your graves, O my people, and brought you up out of your graves,

label: prophetic
text: And shall put my spirit in you, and ye shall live, and I shall place you in your own land: then shall ye know that I the LORD have spoken it, and performed it, saith the LORD.

label: prophetic
text: Thus saith the LORD; For three transgressions of Damascus, and for four, I will not turn away the punishment thereof; because they have threshed Gilead with threshing instruments of iron:

label: prophetic
text: But I will send a fire into the house of Hazael, which shall devour the palaces of Benhadad.

label: prophetic
text: I will break also the bar of Damascus, and cut off the inhabitant from the plain of Aven, and him that holdeth the sceptre from the house of Eden: and the people of Syria shall go into captivity unto Kir, saith the LORD.

label: prophetic
text: But thou, Bethlehem Ephratah, though thou be little among the thousands of Judah, yet out of thee shall he come forth unto me that is to be ruler in Israel; whose goings forth have been from of old, from everlasting.

label: prophetic
text: Therefore will he give them up, until the time that she which travaileth hath brought forth: then the remnant of his brethren shall return unto the children of Israel.

label: prophetic
text: And he shall stand and feed in the strength of the LORD, in the majesty of the name of the LORD his God; and they shall abide: for now shall he be great unto the ends of the earth.

label: prophetic
note: 'I will pour out my spirit' oracle
text: And it shall come to pass afterward, that I will pour out my spirit upon all flesh; and your sons and your daughters shall prophesy, your old men shall dream dreams, your young men shall see visions:

label: prophetic
note: 'I will pour out my spirit' oracle
text: And also upon the servants and upon the handmaids in those days will I pour out my spirit.

label: prophetic
note: 'I will pour out my spirit' oracle
text: And I will shew wonders in the heavens and in the earth, blood, and fire, and pillars of smoke.

label: prophetic
note: 'I will pour out my spirit' oracle
text: The sun shall be turned into darkness, and the moon into blood, before the great and the terrible day of the LORD come.

label: prophetic
note: 'I will pour out my spirit' oracle
text: And it shall come to pass, that whosoever shall call on the name of the LORD shall be delivered: for in mount Zion and in Jerusalem shall be deliverance, as the LORD hath said, and in the remnant whom the LORD shall call.

label: prophetic
text: The great day of the LORD is near, it is near, and hasteth greatly, even the voice of the day of the LORD: the mighty man shall cry there bitterly.

label: prophetic
text: That day is a day of wrath, a day of trouble and distress, a day of wasteness and desolation, a day of darkness and gloominess, a day of clouds and thick darkness,

label: prophetic
text: A day of the trumpet and alarm against the fenced cities, and against the high towers.

label: prophetic
text: And I will bring distress upon men, that they shall walk like blind men, because they have sinned against the LORD: and their blood shall be poured out as dust, and their flesh as the dung.

label: prophetic
text: Neither their silver nor their gold shall be able to deliver them in the day of the LORD’s wrath; but the whole land shall be devoured by the fire of his jealousy: for he shall make even a speedy riddance of all them that dwell in the land.

label: prophetic
text: And the LORD answered me, and said, Write the vision, and make it plain upon tables, that he may run that readeth it.

label: prophetic
text: For the vision is yet for an appointed time, but at the end it shall speak, and not lie: though it tarry, wait for it; because it will surely come, it will not tarry.

label: prophetic
text: Behold, his soul which is lifted up is not upright in him: but the just shall live by his faith.

label: prophetic
text: Rejoice greatly, O daughter of Zion; shout, O daughter of Jerusalem: behold, thy King cometh unto thee: he is just, and having salvation; lowly, and riding upon an ass, and upon a colt the foal of an ass.

label: prophetic
text: And I will cut off the chariot from Ephraim, and the horse from Jerusalem, and the battle bow shall be cut off: and he shall speak peace unto the heathen: and his dominion shall be from sea even to sea, and from the river even to the ends of the earth.

label: prophetic
text: Behold, I will send my messenger, and he shall prepare the way before me: and the LORD, whom ye seek, shall suddenly come to his temple, even the messenger of the covenant, whom ye delight in: behold, he shall come, saith the LORD of hosts.

label: prophetic
text: But who may abide the day of his coming? and who shall stand when he appeareth? for he is like a refiner’s fire, and like fullers’ soap:

label: prophetic
text: And he shall sit as a refiner and purifier of silver: and he shall purify the sons of Levi, and purge them as gold and silver, that they may offer unto the LORD an offering in righteousness.

label: prophetic
text: Seventy weeks are determined upon thy people and upon thy holy city, to finish the transgression, and to make an end of sins, and to make reconciliation for iniquity, and to bring in everlasting righteousness, and to seal up the vision and prophecy, and to anoint the most Holy.

label: prophetic
text: Know therefore and understand, that from the going forth of the commandment to restore and to build Jerusalem unto the Messiah the Prince shall be seven weeks, and threescore and two weeks: the street shall be built again, and the wall, even in troublous times.

label: prophetic
text: And after threescore and two weeks shall Messiah be cut off, but not for himself: and the people of the prince that shall come shall destroy the city and the sanctuary; and the end thereof shall be with a flood, and unto the end of the war desolations are determined.

label: prophetic
text: And he shall confirm the covenant with many for one week: and in the midst of the week he shall cause the sacrifice and the oblation to cease, and for the overspreading of abominations he shall make it desolate, even until the consummation, and that determined shall be poured upon the desolate.

label: prophetic
text: My God, my God, why hast thou forsaken me? why art thou so far from helping me, and from the words of my roaring?

label: prophetic
text: O my God, I cry in the day time, but thou hearest not; and in the night season, and am not silent.

label: prophetic
text: But thou art holy, O thou that inhabitest the praises of Israel.

label: prophetic
text: Our fathers trusted in thee: they trusted, and thou didst deliver them.

label: prophetic
text: They cried unto thee, and were delivered: they trusted in thee, and were not confounded.

label: prophetic
text: But I am a worm, and no man; a reproach of men, and despised of the people.

label: prophetic
text: All they that see me laugh me to scorn: they shoot out the lip, they shake the head, saying,

label: prophetic
text: He trusted on the LORD that he would deliver him: let him deliver him, seeing he delighted in him.

label: prophetic
text: But thou art he that took me out of the womb: thou didst make me hope when I was upon my mother’s breasts.

label: prophetic
text: I was cast upon thee from the womb: thou art my God from my mother’s belly.

label: prophetic
text: Be not far from me; for trouble is near; for there is none to help.

label: prophetic
text: Many bulls have compassed me: strong bulls of Bashan have beset me round.

label: prophetic
text: They gaped upon me with their mouths, as a ravening and a roaring lion.

label: prophetic
text: I am poured out like water, and all my bones are out of joint: my heart is like wax; it is melted in the midst of my bowels.

label: prophetic
text: My strength is dried up like a potsherd; and my tongue cleaveth to my jaws; and thou hast brought me into the dust of death.

label: prophetic
text: For dogs have compassed me: the assembly of the wicked have inclosed me: they pierced my hands and my feet.

label: prophetic
text: I may tell all my bones: they look and stare upon me.

label: prophetic
text: They part my garments among them, and cast lots upon my vesture.

label: prophetic
note: Enoch's judgment oracle, explicitly framed as prophecy; near-verbatim quote of 1 Enoch 1:9
text: And Enoch also, the seventh from Adam, prophesied of these, saying, Behold, the Lord cometh with ten thousands of his saints,

label: prophetic
note: Enoch's judgment oracle, explicitly framed as prophecy; near-verbatim quote of 1 Enoch 1:9
text: To execute judgment upon all, and to convince all that are ungodly among them of all their ungodly deeds which they have ungodly committed, and of all their hard speeches which ungodly sinners have spoken against him.

label: prophetic
note: angel of the LORD's birth-oracle to Hagar (Ishmael)
text: And the angel of the LORD said unto her, Return to thy mistress, and submit thyself under her hands.

label: prophetic
note: angel of the LORD's birth-oracle to Hagar (Ishmael)
text: And the angel of the LORD said unto her, I will multiply thy seed exceedingly, that it shall not be numbered for multitude.

label: prophetic
note: angel of the LORD's birth-oracle to Hagar (Ishmael)
text: And the angel of the LORD said unto her, Behold, thou art with child, and shalt bear a son, and shalt call his name Ishmael; because the LORD hath heard thy affliction.

label: prophetic
note: angel of the LORD's birth-oracle to Hagar (Ishmael)
text: And he will be a wild man; his hand will be against every man, and every man’s hand against him; and he shall dwell in the presence of all his brethren.

label: prophetic
note: angel of the LORD: 'saith the LORD' covenant/blessing oracle to Abraham
text: And said, By myself have I sworn, saith the LORD, for because thou hast done this thing, and hast not withheld thy son, thine only son:

label: prophetic
note: angel of the LORD: 'saith the LORD' covenant/blessing oracle to Abraham
text: That in blessing I will bless thee, and in multiplying I will multiply thy seed as the stars of the heaven, and as the sand which is upon the sea shore; and thy seed shall possess the gate of his enemies;

label: prophetic
note: angel of the LORD: 'saith the LORD' covenant/blessing oracle to Abraham
text: And in thy seed shall all the nations of the earth be blessed; because thou hast obeyed my voice.

label: prophetic
note: burning bush: divine self-revelation to Moses
text: And when the LORD saw that he turned aside to see, God called unto him out of the midst of the bush, and said, Moses, Moses. And he said, Here am I.

label: prophetic
note: burning bush: divine self-revelation to Moses
text: And he said, Draw not nigh hither: put off thy shoes from off thy feet, for the place whereon thou standest is holy ground.

label: prophetic
note: burning bush: divine self-revelation to Moses
text: Moreover he said, I am the God of thy father, the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob. And Moses hid his face; for he was afraid to look upon God.

label: prophetic
note: angel of the LORD commissions Gideon: 'Go in this thy might'
text: And the LORD looked upon him, and said, Go in this thy might, and thou shalt save Israel from the hand of the Midianites: have not I sent thee?

label: prophetic
note: angel of the LORD to Gideon: 'Surely I will be with thee'
text: And the LORD said unto him, Surely I will be with thee, and thou shalt smite the Midianites as one man.

label: prophetic
note: angel of the LORD to Gideon: 'Peace be unto thee; fear not'
text: And the LORD said unto him, Peace be unto thee; fear not: thou shalt not die.

label: prophetic
note: angel of the LORD's birth-oracle to Manoah's wife (Samson)
text: And the angel of the LORD appeared unto the woman, and said unto her, Behold now, thou art barren, and bearest not: but thou shalt conceive, and bear a son.

label: prophetic
note: angel of the LORD's birth-oracle to Manoah's wife (Samson)
text: Now therefore beware, I pray thee, and drink not wine nor strong drink, and eat not any unclean thing:

label: prophetic
note: angel of the LORD's birth-oracle to Manoah's wife (Samson)
text: For, lo, thou shalt conceive, and bear a son; and no razor shall come on his head: for the child shall be a Nazarite unto God from the womb: and he shall begin to deliver Israel out of the hand of the Philistines.

label: prophetic
note: Sinai: divine self-declaration opening the Decalogue, 'I am the LORD thy God'
text: And God spake all these words, saying,

label: prophetic
note: Sinai: divine self-declaration opening the Decalogue, 'I am the LORD thy God'
text: I am the LORD thy God, which have brought thee out of the land of Egypt, out of the house of bondage.

label: prophetic
note: Sinai-theophany imagery reused as judgment oracle: 'the LORD cometh forth... mountains molten'
text: For, behold, the LORD cometh forth out of his place, and will come down, and tread upon the high places of the earth.

label: prophetic
note: Sinai-theophany imagery reused as judgment oracle: 'the LORD cometh forth... mountains molten'
text: And the mountains shall be molten under him, and the valleys shall be cleft, as wax before the fire, and as the waters that are poured down a steep place.

label: narrative
note: Enoch: 'walked with God ... and he was not'
text: And Enoch lived sixty and five years, and begat Methuselah:

label: narrative
note: Enoch: 'walked with God ... and he was not'
text: And Enoch walked with God after he begat Methuselah three hundred years, and begat sons and daughters:

label: narrative
note: Enoch: 'walked with God ... and he was not'
text: And all the days of Enoch were three hundred sixty and five years:

label: narrative
note: Enoch: 'walked with God ... and he was not'
text: And Enoch walked with God: and he was not; for God took him.

label: narrative
note: Abram's call and journey (narrated, not the oracle verses)
text: Now the LORD had said unto Abram, Get thee out of thy country, and from thy kindred, and from thy father’s house, unto a land that I will shew thee:

label: narrative
note: Abram's call and journey (narrated, not the oracle verses)
text: And I will make of thee a great nation, and I will bless thee, and make thy name great; and thou shalt be a blessing:

label: narrative
note: Abram's call and journey (narrated, not the oracle verses)
text: And I will bless them that bless thee, and curse him that curseth thee: and in thee shall all families of the earth be blessed.

label: narrative
note: Abram's call and journey (narrated, not the oracle verses)
text: So Abram departed, as the LORD had spoken unto him; and Lot went with him: and Abram was seventy and five years old when he departed out of Haran.

label: narrative
note: Abram's call and journey (narrated, not the oracle verses)
text: And Abram took Sarai his wife, and Lot his brother’s son, and all their substance that they had gathered, and the souls that they had gotten in Haran; and they went forth to go into the land of Canaan; and into the land of Canaan they came.

label: narrative
note: Abram's call and journey (narrated, not the oracle verses)
text: And Abram passed through the land unto the place of Sichem, unto the plain of Moreh. And the Canaanite was then in the land.

label: narrative
note: Abram's call and journey (narrated, not the oracle verses)
text: And the LORD appeared unto Abram, and said, Unto thy seed will I give this land: and there builded he an altar unto the LORD, who appeared unto him.

label: narrative
note: Abram's call and journey (narrated, not the oracle verses)
text: And he removed from thence unto a mountain on the east of Bethel, and pitched his tent, having Bethel on the west, and Hai on the east: and there he builded an altar unto the LORD, and called upon the name of the LORD.

label: narrative
note: Abram's call and journey (narrated, not the oracle verses)
text: And Abram journeyed, going on still toward the south.

label: narrative
note: Isaac and Rebekah at the well
text: And it came to pass, before he had done speaking, that, behold, Rebekah came out, who was born to Bethuel, son of Milcah, the wife of Nahor, Abraham’s brother, with her pitcher upon her shoulder.

label: narrative
note: Isaac and Rebekah at the well
text: And the damsel was very fair to look upon, a virgin, neither had any man known her: and she went down to the well, and filled her pitcher, and came up.

label: narrative
note: Isaac and Rebekah at the well
text: And the servant ran to meet her, and said, Let me, I pray thee, drink a little water of thy pitcher.

label: narrative
note: Isaac and Rebekah at the well
text: And she said, Drink, my lord: and she hasted, and let down her pitcher upon her hand, and gave him drink.

label: narrative
note: Isaac and Rebekah at the well
text: And when she had done giving him drink, she said, I will draw water for thy camels also, until they have done drinking.

label: narrative
note: Isaac and Rebekah at the well
text: And she hasted, and emptied her pitcher into the trough, and ran again unto the well to draw water, and drew for all his camels.

label: narrative
note: Isaac and Rebekah at the well
text: And the man wondering at her held his peace, to wit whether the LORD had made his journey prosperous or not.

label: narrative
note: Isaac and Rebekah at the well
text: And it came to pass, as the camels had done drinking, that the man took a golden earring of half a shekel weight, and two bracelets for her hands of ten shekels weight of gold;

label: narrative
note: Isaac and Rebekah at the well
text: And said, Whose daughter art thou? tell me, I pray thee: is there room in thy father’s house for us to lodge in?

label: narrative
note: Isaac and Rebekah at the well
text: And she said unto him, I am the daughter of Bethuel the son of Milcah, which she bare unto Nahor.

label: narrative
note: Isaac and Rebekah at the well
text: She said moreover unto him, We have both straw and provender enough, and room to lodge in.

label: narrative
note: Isaac and Rebekah at the well
text: And the man bowed down his head, and worshipped the LORD.

label: narrative
note: Isaac and Rebekah at the well
text: And he said, Blessed be the LORD God of my master Abraham, who hath not left destitute my master of his mercy and his truth: I being in the way, the LORD led me to the house of my master’s brethren.

label: narrative
text: And it came to pass, when Joseph was come unto his brethren, that they stript Joseph out of his coat, his coat of many colours that was on him;

label: narrative
text: And they took him, and cast him into a pit: and the pit was empty, there was no water in it.

label: narrative
text: And they sat down to eat bread: and they lifted up their eyes and looked, and, behold, a company of Ishmeelites came from Gilead with their camels bearing spicery and balm and myrrh, going to carry it down to Egypt.

label: narrative
text: And Judah said unto his brethren, What profit is it if we slay our brother, and conceal his blood?

label: narrative
text: Come, and let us sell him to the Ishmeelites, and let not our hand be upon him; for he is our brother and our flesh. And his brethren were content.

label: narrative
text: Then there passed by Midianites merchantmen; and they drew and lifted up Joseph out of the pit, and sold Joseph to the Ishmeelites for twenty pieces of silver: and they brought Joseph into Egypt.

label: narrative
note: angel of the LORD finds Hagar (narrative frame, no oracle yet)
text: And the angel of the LORD found her by a fountain of water in the wilderness, by the fountain in the way to Shur.

label: narrative
note: angel of the LORD finds Hagar (narrative frame, no oracle yet)
text: And he said, Hagar, Sarai’s maid, whence camest thou? and whither wilt thou go? And she said, I flee from the face of my mistress Sarai.

label: narrative
text: And she called the name of the LORD that spake unto her, Thou God seest me: for she said, Have I also here looked after him that seeth me?

label: narrative
note: angel of the LORD calls 'Abraham, Abraham' (bare address, no oracle content)
text: And the angel of the LORD called unto him out of heaven, and said, Abraham, Abraham: and he said, Here am I.

label: narrative
note: angel of the LORD appears in the burning bush (narrative frame)
text: And the angel of the LORD appeared unto him in a flame of fire out of the midst of a bush: and he looked, and, behold, the bush burned with fire, and the bush was not consumed.

label: narrative
note: angel of the LORD appears in the burning bush (narrative frame)
text: And Moses said, I will now turn aside, and see this great sight, why the bush is not burnt.

label: narrative
note: angel of the LORD sits under the oak; Gideon's skeptical reply
text: And there came an angel of the LORD, and sat under an oak which was in Ophrah, that pertained unto Joash the Abiezrite: and his son Gideon threshed wheat by the winepress, to hide it from the Midianites.

label: narrative
note: angel of the LORD sits under the oak; Gideon's skeptical reply
text: And the angel of the LORD appeared unto him, and said unto him, The LORD is with thee, thou mighty man of valour.

label: narrative
note: angel of the LORD sits under the oak; Gideon's skeptical reply
text: And Gideon said unto him, Oh my Lord, if the LORD be with us, why then is all this befallen us? and where be all his miracles which our fathers told us of, saying, Did not the LORD bring us up from Egypt? but now the LORD hath forsaken us, and delivered us into the hands of the Midianites.

label: narrative
note: angel of the LORD ascends in the altar flame (narrative, no speech)
text: So Manoah took a kid with a meat offering, and offered it upon a rock unto the LORD: and the angel did wonderously; and Manoah and his wife looked on.

label: narrative
note: angel of the LORD ascends in the altar flame (narrative, no speech)
text: For it came to pass, when the flame went up toward heaven from off the altar, that the angel of the LORD ascended in the flame of the altar. And Manoah and his wife looked on it, and fell on their faces to the ground.

label: narrative
note: angel of the LORD ascends in the altar flame (narrative, no speech)
text: But the angel of the LORD did no more appear to Manoah and to his wife. Then Manoah knew that he was an angel of the LORD.

label: narrative
note: Sinai theophany: thunder, cloud, trumpet, quaking mountain (narrative frame, no direct speech to the people yet)
text: And it came to pass on the third day in the morning, that there were thunders and lightnings, and a thick cloud upon the mount, and the voice of the trumpet exceeding loud; so that all the people that was in the camp trembled.

label: narrative
note: Sinai theophany: thunder, cloud, trumpet, quaking mountain (narrative frame, no direct speech to the people yet)
text: And Moses brought forth the people out of the camp to meet with God; and they stood at the nether part of the mount.

label: narrative
note: Sinai theophany: thunder, cloud, trumpet, quaking mountain (narrative frame, no direct speech to the people yet)
text: And mount Sinai was altogether on a smoke, because the LORD descended upon it in fire: and the smoke thereof ascended as the smoke of a furnace, and the whole mount quaked greatly.

label: narrative
note: Sinai theophany: thunder, cloud, trumpet, quaking mountain (narrative frame, no direct speech to the people yet)
text: And when the voice of the trumpet sounded long, and waxed louder and louder, Moses spake, and God answered him by a voice.

label: narrative
note: Sinai theophany: thunder, cloud, trumpet, quaking mountain (narrative frame, no direct speech to the people yet)
text: And the LORD came down upon mount Sinai, on the top of the mount: and the LORD called Moses up to the top of the mount; and Moses went up.

label: narrative
text: Now it came to pass in the days when the judges ruled, that there was a famine in the land. And a certain man of Bethlehemjudah went to sojourn in the country of Moab, he, and his wife, and his two sons.

label: narrative
text: And the name of the man was Elimelech, and the name of his wife Naomi, and the name of his two sons Mahlon and Chilion, Ephrathites of Bethlehemjudah. And they came into the country of Moab, and continued there.

label: narrative
text: And Elimelech Naomi’s husband died; and she was left, and her two sons.

label: narrative
text: And they took them wives of the women of Moab; the name of the one was Orpah, and the name of the other Ruth: and they dwelled there about ten years.

label: narrative
text: And Mahlon and Chilion died also both of them; and the woman was left of her two sons and her husband.

label: narrative
text: Then she arose with her daughters in law, that she might return from the country of Moab: for she had heard in the country of Moab how that the LORD had visited his people in giving them bread.

label: narrative
text: Wherefore she went forth out of the place where she was, and her two daughters in law with her; and they went on the way to return unto the land of Judah.

label: narrative
text: And Naomi said unto her two daughters in law, Go, return each to her mother’s house: the LORD deal kindly with you, as ye have dealt with the dead, and with me.

label: narrative
text: The LORD grant you that ye may find rest, each of you in the house of her husband. Then she kissed them; and they lifted up their voice, and wept.

label: narrative
text: And they said unto her, Surely we will return with thee unto thy people.

label: narrative
text: And Naomi said, Turn again, my daughters: why will ye go with me? are there yet any more sons in my womb, that they may be your husbands?

label: narrative
text: Turn again, my daughters, go your way; for I am too old to have an husband. If I should say, I have hope, if I should have an husband also to night, and should also bear sons;

label: narrative
text: Would ye tarry for them till they were grown? would ye stay for them from having husbands? nay, my daughters; for it grieveth me much for your sakes that the hand of the LORD is gone out against me.

label: narrative
text: And they lifted up their voice, and wept again: and Orpah kissed her mother in law; but Ruth clave unto her.

label: narrative
text: And she said, Behold, thy sister in law is gone back unto her people, and unto her gods: return thou after thy sister in law.

label: narrative
text: And Ruth said, Intreat me not to leave thee, or to return from following after thee: for whither thou goest, I will go; and where thou lodgest, I will lodge: thy people shall be my people, and thy God my God:

label: narrative
text: Where thou diest, will I die, and there will I be buried: the LORD do so to me, and more also, if ought but death part thee and me.

label: narrative
text: When she saw that she was stedfastly minded to go with her, then she left speaking unto her.

label: narrative
text: And Boaz said unto the elders, and unto all the people, Ye are witnesses this day, that I have bought all that was Elimelech’s, and all that was Chilion’s and Mahlon’s, of the hand of Naomi.

label: narrative
text: Moreover Ruth the Moabitess, the wife of Mahlon, have I purchased to be my wife, to raise up the name of the dead upon his inheritance, that the name of the dead be not cut off from among his brethren, and from the gate of his place: ye are witnesses this day.

label: narrative
text: And all the people that were in the gate, and the elders, said, We are witnesses. The LORD make the woman that is come into thine house like Rachel and like Leah, which two did build the house of Israel: and do thou worthily in Ephratah, and be famous in Bethlehem:

label: narrative
text: And let thy house be like the house of Pharez, whom Tamar bare unto Judah, of the seed which the LORD shall give thee of this young woman.

label: narrative
text: So Boaz took Ruth, and she was his wife: and when he went in unto her, the LORD gave her conception, and she bare a son.

label: narrative
text: And the women said unto Naomi, Blessed be the LORD, which hath not left thee this day without a kinsman, that his name may be famous in Israel.

label: narrative
text: And he shall be unto thee a restorer of thy life, and a nourisher of thine old age: for thy daughter in law, which loveth thee, which is better to thee than seven sons, hath born him.

label: narrative
text: And Naomi took the child, and laid it in her bosom, and became nurse unto it.

label: narrative
text: And the women her neighbours gave it a name, saying, There is a son born to Naomi; and they called his name Obed: he is the father of Jesse, the father of David.

label: narrative
text: Now when the turn of Esther, the daughter of Abihail the uncle of Mordecai, who had taken her for his daughter, was come to go in unto the king, she required nothing but what Hegai the king’s chamberlain, the keeper of the women, appointed. And Esther obtained favour in the sight of all them that looked upon her.

label: narrative
text: So Esther was taken unto king Ahasuerus into his house royal in the tenth month, which is the month Tebeth, in the seventh year of his reign.

label: narrative
text: And the king loved Esther above all the women, and she obtained grace and favour in his sight more than all the virgins; so that he set the royal crown upon her head, and made her queen instead of Vashti.

label: narrative
text: Then the king made a great feast unto all his princes and his servants, even Esther’s feast; and he made a release to the provinces, and gave gifts, according to the state of the king.

label: narrative
text: So the king and Haman came to banquet with Esther the queen.

label: narrative
text: And the king said again unto Esther on the second day at the banquet of wine, What is thy petition, queen Esther? and it shall be granted thee: and what is thy request? and it shall be performed, even to the half of the kingdom.

label: narrative
text: Then Esther the queen answered and said, If I have found favour in thy sight, O king, and if it please the king, let my life be given me at my petition, and my people at my request:

label: narrative
text: For we are sold, I and my people, to be destroyed, to be slain, and to perish. But if we had been sold for bondmen and bondwomen, I had held my tongue, although the enemy could not countervail the king’s damage.

label: narrative
text: Then the king Ahasuerus answered and said unto Esther the queen, Who is he, and where is he, that durst presume in his heart to do so?

label: narrative
text: And Esther said, The adversary and enemy is this wicked Haman. Then Haman was afraid before the king and the queen.

label: narrative
text: And the king arising from the banquet of wine in his wrath went into the palace garden: and Haman stood up to make request for his life to Esther the queen; for he saw that there was evil determined against him by the king.

label: narrative
text: Then the king returned out of the palace garden into the place of the banquet of wine; and Haman was fallen upon the bed whereon Esther was. Then said the king, Will he force the queen also before me in the house? As the word went out of the king’s mouth, they covered Haman’s face.

label: narrative
text: And Harbonah, one of the chamberlains, said before the king, Behold also, the gallows fifty cubits high, which Haman had made for Mordecai, who had spoken good for the king, standeth in the house of Haman. Then the king said, Hang him thereon.

label: narrative
text: So they hanged Haman on the gallows that he had prepared for Mordecai. Then was the king’s wrath pacified.

label: narrative
text: Then came there two women, that were harlots, unto the king, and stood before him.

label: narrative
text: And the one woman said, O my lord, I and this woman dwell in one house; and I was delivered of a child with her in the house.

label: narrative
text: And it came to pass the third day after that I was delivered, that this woman was delivered also: and we were together; there was no stranger with us in the house, save we two in the house.

label: narrative
text: And this woman’s child died in the night; because she overlaid it.

label: narrative
text: And she arose at midnight, and took my son from beside me, while thine handmaid slept, and laid it in her bosom, and laid her dead child in my bosom.

label: narrative
text: And when I rose in the morning to give my child suck, behold, it was dead: but when I had considered it in the morning, behold, it was not my son, which I did bear.

label: narrative
text: And the other woman said, Nay; but the living is my son, and the dead is thy son. And this said, No; but the dead is thy son, and the living is my son. Thus they spake before the king.

label: narrative
text: Then said the king, The one saith, This is my son that liveth, and thy son is the dead: and the other saith, Nay; but thy son is the dead, and my son is the living.

label: narrative
text: And the king said, Bring me a sword. And they brought a sword before the king.

label: narrative
text: And the king said, Divide the living child in two, and give half to the one, and half to the other.

label: narrative
text: Then spake the woman whose the living child was unto the king, for her bowels yearned upon her son, and she said, O my lord, give her the living child, and in no wise slay it. But the other said, Let it be neither mine nor thine, but divide it.

label: narrative
text: Then the king answered and said, Give her the living child, and in no wise slay it: she is the mother thereof.

label: narrative
text: And all Israel heard of the judgment which the king had judged; and they feared the king: for they saw that the wisdom of God was in him, to do judgment.

label: narrative
text: Now Naaman, captain of the host of the king of Syria, was a great man with his master, and honourable, because by him the LORD had given deliverance unto Syria: he was also a mighty man in valour, but he was a leper.

label: narrative
text: And the Syrians had gone out by companies, and had brought away captive out of the land of Israel a little maid; and she waited on Naaman’s wife.

label: narrative
text: And she said unto her mistress, Would God my lord were with the prophet that is in Samaria! for he would recover him of his leprosy.

label: narrative
text: And one went in, and told his lord, saying, Thus and thus said the maid that is of the land of Israel.

label: narrative
text: And the king of Syria said, Go to, go, and I will send a letter unto the king of Israel. And he departed, and took with him ten talents of silver, and six thousand pieces of gold, and ten changes of raiment.

label: narrative
text: And he brought the letter to the king of Israel, saying, Now when this letter is come unto thee, behold, I have therewith sent Naaman my servant to thee, that thou mayest recover him of his leprosy.

label: narrative
text: And it came to pass, when the king of Israel had read the letter, that he rent his clothes, and said, Am I God, to kill and to make alive, that this man doth send unto me to recover a man of his leprosy? wherefore consider, I pray you, and see how he seeketh a quarrel against me.

label: narrative
text: And it was so, when Elisha the man of God had heard that the king of Israel had rent his clothes, that he sent to the king, saying, Wherefore hast thou rent thy clothes? let him come now to me, and he shall know that there is a prophet in Israel.

label: narrative
text: So Naaman came with his horses and with his chariot, and stood at the door of the house of Elisha.

label: narrative
text: And Elisha sent a messenger unto him, saying, Go and wash in Jordan seven times, and thy flesh shall come again to thee, and thou shalt be clean.

label: narrative
text: But Naaman was wroth, and went away, and said, Behold, I thought, He will surely come out to me, and stand, and call on the name of the LORD his God, and strike his hand over the place, and recover the leper.

label: narrative
text: Are not Abana and Pharpar, rivers of Damascus, better than all the waters of Israel? may I not wash in them, and be clean? So he turned and went away in a rage.

label: narrative
text: And his servants came near, and spake unto him, and said, My father, if the prophet had bid thee do some great thing, wouldest thou not have done it? how much rather then, when he saith to thee, Wash, and be clean?

label: narrative
text: Then went he down, and dipped himself seven times in Jordan, according to the saying of the man of God: and his flesh came again like unto the flesh of a little child, and he was clean.

label: narrative
text: Josiah was eight years old when he began to reign, and he reigned in Jerusalem one and thirty years.

label: narrative
text: And he did that which was right in the sight of the LORD, and walked in the ways of David his father, and declined neither to the right hand, nor to the left.

label: narrative
text: For in the eighth year of his reign, while he was yet young, he began to seek after the God of David his father: and in the twelfth year he began to purge Judah and Jerusalem from the high places, and the groves, and the carved images, and the molten images.

label: narrative
text: And they brake down the altars of Baalim in his presence; and the images, that were on high above them, he cut down; and the groves, and the carved images, and the molten images, he brake in pieces, and made dust of them, and strowed it upon the graves of them that had sacrificed unto them.

label: narrative
text: And he burnt the bones of the priests upon their altars, and cleansed Judah and Jerusalem.

label: narrative
text: And so did he in the cities of Manasseh, and Ephraim, and Simeon, even unto Naphtali, with their mattocks round about.

label: narrative
text: And when he had broken down the altars and the groves, and had beaten the graven images into powder, and cut down all the idols throughout all the land of Israel, he returned to Jerusalem.

label: narrative
text: Now in the eighteenth year of his reign, when he had purged the land, and the house, he sent Shaphan the son of Azaliah, and Maaseiah the governor of the city, and Joah the son of Joahaz the recorder, to repair the house of the LORD his God.

label: narrative
text: And when they came to Hilkiah the high priest, they delivered the money that was brought into the house of God, which the Levites that kept the doors had gathered of the hand of Manasseh and Ephraim, and of all the remnant of Israel, and of all Judah and Benjamin; and they returned to Jerusalem.

label: narrative
text: And they put it in the hand of the workmen that had the oversight of the house of the LORD, and they gave it to the workmen that wrought in the house of the LORD, to repair and amend the house:

label: narrative
text: Even to the artificers and builders gave they it, to buy hewn stone, and timber for couplings, and to floor the houses which the kings of Judah had destroyed.

label: narrative
text: And the men did the work faithfully: and the overseers of them were Jahath and Obadiah, the Levites, of the sons of Merari; and Zechariah and Meshullam, of the sons of the Kohathites, to set it forward; and other of the Levites, all that could skill of instruments of musick.

label: narrative
text: Also they were over the bearers of burdens, and were overseers of all that wrought the work in any manner of service: and of the Levites there were scribes, and officers, and porters.

label: law-wisdom
note: the Ten Commandments proper, split from the Sinai divine-speech opening above
text: Thou shalt have no other gods before me.

label: law-wisdom
note: the Ten Commandments proper, split from the Sinai divine-speech opening above
text: Thou shalt not make unto thee any graven image, or any likeness of any thing that is in heaven above, or that is in the earth beneath, or that is in the water under the earth:

label: law-wisdom
note: the Ten Commandments proper, split from the Sinai divine-speech opening above
text: Thou shalt not bow down thyself to them, nor serve them: for I the LORD thy God am a jealous God, visiting the iniquity of the fathers upon the children unto the third and fourth generation of them that hate me;

label: law-wisdom
note: the Ten Commandments proper, split from the Sinai divine-speech opening above
text: And shewing mercy unto thousands of them that love me, and keep my commandments.

label: law-wisdom
note: the Ten Commandments proper, split from the Sinai divine-speech opening above
text: Thou shalt not take the name of the LORD thy God in vain; for the LORD will not hold him guiltless that taketh his name in vain.

label: law-wisdom
note: the Ten Commandments proper, split from the Sinai divine-speech opening above
text: Remember the sabbath day, to keep it holy.

label: law-wisdom
note: the Ten Commandments proper, split from the Sinai divine-speech opening above
text: Six days shalt thou labour, and do all thy work:

label: law-wisdom
note: the Ten Commandments proper, split from the Sinai divine-speech opening above
text: But the seventh day is the sabbath of the LORD thy God: in it thou shalt not do any work, thou, nor thy son, nor thy daughter, thy manservant, nor thy maidservant, nor thy cattle, nor thy stranger that is within thy gates:

label: law-wisdom
note: the Ten Commandments proper, split from the Sinai divine-speech opening above
text: For in six days the LORD made heaven and earth, the sea, and all that in them is, and rested the seventh day: wherefore the LORD blessed the sabbath day, and hallowed it.

label: law-wisdom
note: the Ten Commandments proper, split from the Sinai divine-speech opening above
text: Honour thy father and thy mother: that thy days may be long upon the land which the LORD thy God giveth thee.

label: law-wisdom
note: the Ten Commandments proper, split from the Sinai divine-speech opening above
text: Thou shalt not kill.

label: law-wisdom
note: the Ten Commandments proper, split from the Sinai divine-speech opening above
text: Thou shalt not commit adultery.

label: law-wisdom
note: the Ten Commandments proper, split from the Sinai divine-speech opening above
text: Thou shalt not steal.

label: law-wisdom
note: the Ten Commandments proper, split from the Sinai divine-speech opening above
text: Thou shalt not bear false witness against thy neighbour.

label: law-wisdom
note: the Ten Commandments proper, split from the Sinai divine-speech opening above
text: Thou shalt not covet thy neighbour’s house, thou shalt not covet thy neighbour’s wife, nor his manservant, nor his maidservant, nor his ox, nor his ass, nor any thing that is thy neighbour’s.

label: law-wisdom
text: And the LORD spake unto Moses and to Aaron, saying unto them,

label: law-wisdom
text: Speak unto the children of Israel, saying, These are the beasts which ye shall eat among all the beasts that are on the earth.

label: law-wisdom
text: Whatsoever parteth the hoof, and is clovenfooted, and cheweth the cud, among the beasts, that shall ye eat.

label: law-wisdom
text: Nevertheless these shall ye not eat of them that chew the cud, or of them that divide the hoof: as the camel, because he cheweth the cud, but divideth not the hoof; he is unclean unto you.

label: law-wisdom
text: And the coney, because he cheweth the cud, but divideth not the hoof; he is unclean unto you.

label: law-wisdom
text: And the hare, because he cheweth the cud, but divideth not the hoof; he is unclean unto you.

label: law-wisdom
text: And the swine, though he divide the hoof, and be clovenfooted, yet he cheweth not the cud; he is unclean to you.

label: law-wisdom
text: Of their flesh shall ye not eat, and their carcase shall ye not touch; they are unclean to you.

label: law-wisdom
text: And when ye reap the harvest of your land, thou shalt not wholly reap the corners of thy field, neither shalt thou gather the gleanings of thy harvest.

label: law-wisdom
text: And thou shalt not glean thy vineyard, neither shalt thou gather every grape of thy vineyard; thou shalt leave them for the poor and stranger: I am the LORD your God.

label: law-wisdom
text: Ye shall not steal, neither deal falsely, neither lie one to another.

label: law-wisdom
text: And ye shall not swear by my name falsely, neither shalt thou profane the name of thy God: I am the LORD.

label: law-wisdom
text: Thou shalt not defraud thy neighbour, neither rob him: the wages of him that is hired shall not abide with thee all night until the morning.

label: law-wisdom
text: Thou shalt not curse the deaf, nor put a stumblingblock before the blind, but shalt fear thy God: I am the LORD.

label: law-wisdom
text: Ye shall do no unrighteousness in judgment: thou shalt not respect the person of the poor, nor honor the person of the mighty: but in righteousness shalt thou judge thy neighbour.

label: law-wisdom
text: Thou shalt not go up and down as a talebearer among thy people: neither shalt thou stand against the blood of thy neighbour: I am the LORD.

label: law-wisdom
text: Thou shalt not hate thy brother in thine heart: thou shalt in any wise rebuke thy neighbour, and not suffer sin upon him.

label: law-wisdom
text: Thou shalt not avenge, nor bear any grudge against the children of thy people, but thou shalt love thy neighbour as thyself: I am the LORD.

label: law-wisdom
text: And the LORD spake unto Moses, saying,

label: law-wisdom
text: Speak unto the children of Israel, and say unto them, Concerning the feasts of the LORD, which ye shall proclaim to be holy convocations, even these are my feasts.

label: law-wisdom
text: Six days shall work be done: but the seventh day is the sabbath of rest, an holy convocation; ye shall do no work therein: it is the sabbath of the LORD in all your dwellings.

label: law-wisdom
text: These are the feasts of the LORD, even holy convocations, which ye shall proclaim in their seasons.

label: law-wisdom
text: In the fourteenth day of the first month at even is the LORD’s passover.

label: law-wisdom
text: And on the fifteenth day of the same month is the feast of unleavened bread unto the LORD: seven days ye must eat unleavened bread.

label: law-wisdom
text: In the first day ye shall have an holy convocation: ye shall do no servile work therein.

label: law-wisdom
text: But ye shall offer an offering made by fire unto the LORD seven days: in the seventh day is an holy convocation: ye shall do no servile work therein.

label: law-wisdom
text: The proverbs of Solomon the son of David, king of Israel;

label: law-wisdom
text: To know wisdom and instruction; to perceive the words of understanding;

label: law-wisdom
text: To receive the instruction of wisdom, justice, and judgment, and equity;

label: law-wisdom
text: To give subtilty to the simple, to the young man knowledge and discretion.

label: law-wisdom
text: A wise man will hear, and will increase learning; and a man of understanding shall attain unto wise counsels:

label: law-wisdom
text: To understand a proverb, and the interpretation; the words of the wise, and their dark sayings.

label: law-wisdom
text: The fear of the LORD is the beginning of knowledge: but fools despise wisdom and instruction.

label: law-wisdom
text: My son, forget not my law; but let thine heart keep my commandments:

label: law-wisdom
text: For length of days, and long life, and peace, shall they add to thee.

label: law-wisdom
text: Let not mercy and truth forsake thee: bind them about thy neck; write them upon the table of thine heart:

label: law-wisdom
text: So shalt thou find favour and good understanding in the sight of God and man.

label: law-wisdom
text: Trust in the LORD with all thine heart; and lean not unto thine own understanding.

label: law-wisdom
text: In all thy ways acknowledge him, and he shall direct thy paths.

label: law-wisdom
text: Be not wise in thine own eyes: fear the LORD, and depart from evil.

label: law-wisdom
text: It shall be health to thy navel, and marrow to thy bones.

label: law-wisdom
text: Honour the LORD with thy substance, and with the firstfruits of all thine increase:

label: law-wisdom
text: So shall thy barns be filled with plenty, and thy presses shall burst out with new wine.

label: law-wisdom
text: My son, despise not the chastening of the LORD; neither be weary of his correction:

label: law-wisdom
text: For whom the LORD loveth he correcteth; even as a father the son in whom he delighteth.

label: law-wisdom
text: Go to the ant, thou sluggard; consider her ways, and be wise:

label: law-wisdom
text: Which having no guide, overseer, or ruler,

label: law-wisdom
text: Provideth her meat in the summer, and gathereth her food in the harvest.

label: law-wisdom
text: How long wilt thou sleep, O sluggard? when wilt thou arise out of thy sleep?

label: law-wisdom
text: Yet a little sleep, a little slumber, a little folding of the hands to sleep:

label: law-wisdom
text: So shall thy poverty come as one that travelleth, and thy want as an armed man.

label: law-wisdom
text: Who can find a virtuous woman? for her price is far above rubies.

label: law-wisdom
text: The heart of her husband doth safely trust in her, so that he shall have no need of spoil.

label: law-wisdom
text: She will do him good and not evil all the days of her life.

label: law-wisdom
text: She seeketh wool, and flax, and worketh willingly with her hands.

label: law-wisdom
text: She is like the merchants’ ships; she bringeth her food from afar.

label: law-wisdom
text: She riseth also while it is yet night, and giveth meat to her household, and a portion to her maidens.

label: law-wisdom
text: She considereth a field, and buyeth it: with the fruit of her hands she planteth a vineyard.

label: law-wisdom
text: She girdeth her loins with strength, and strengtheneth her arms.

label: law-wisdom
text: She perceiveth that her merchandise is good: her candle goeth not out by night.

label: law-wisdom
text: She layeth her hands to the spindle, and her hands hold the distaff.

label: law-wisdom
text: She stretcheth out her hand to the poor; yea, she reacheth forth her hands to the needy.

label: law-wisdom
text: She is not afraid of the snow for her household: for all her household are clothed with scarlet.

label: law-wisdom
text: She maketh herself coverings of tapestry; her clothing is silk and purple.

label: law-wisdom
text: Her husband is known in the gates, when he sitteth among the elders of the land.

label: law-wisdom
text: She maketh fine linen, and selleth it; and delivereth girdles unto the merchant.

label: law-wisdom
text: Strength and honour are her clothing; and she shall rejoice in time to come.

label: law-wisdom
text: She openeth her mouth with wisdom; and in her tongue is the law of kindness.

label: law-wisdom
text: She looketh well to the ways of her household, and eateth not the bread of idleness.

label: law-wisdom
text: Her children arise up, and call her blessed; her husband also, and he praiseth her.

label: law-wisdom
text: Many daughters have done virtuously, but thou excellest them all.

label: law-wisdom
text: Favour is deceitful, and beauty is vain: but a woman that feareth the LORD, she shall be praised.

label: law-wisdom
text: Give her of the fruit of her hands; and let her own works praise her in the gates.
